idea.hook: Is the Universe Expanding... Too Fast? 🌌
facts: The universe's expansion rate (Hubble Constant) is crucial for understanding its age & future.
Calculations from the early universe (Cosmic Microwave Background) predict a rate of ~67.4 km/s/Mpc.
But recent observations from the nearby universe (supernovae) consistently measure it as ~73.0 km/s/Mpc.
idea.payoff: This ~8% discrepancy is NOT a measurement error. It's a profound cosmic crisis pointing to NEW, unknown physics!
idea.title: Universe's Speed Problem?! 🤯 #HubbleTension

scenes.text: Is the Universe Expanding... Too Fast? 🌌
scenes.visual: A vast, beautiful nebula with subtle, slow outward expansion animation. Stars twinkle.

scenes.text: Hubble Constant: Key to Universe's Age & Future.
scenes.visual: Stylized graphic of galaxies moving apart, illustrating cosmic expansion. Text appears over this animation.
A swirling, colorful map of the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) with the number '67.4' subtly highlighted.
A distant galaxy cluster, with a bright, brief flash of a supernova explosion. The number '73.0' is subtly highlighted.

scenes.text:
This ~8% gap ISN'T ERROR.
It's NEW, UNKNOWN PHYSICS!
scenes.visual: Two distinct lines/waves representing the conflicting measurements diverge, then transition to a dramatic, mysterious cosmic void with swirling dark energy effects and faint, unknown symbols.
